content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

August 6, 2002

Leslie Cook, CMC
City Clerk
City of Santa Cruz
809 Center Street, Room 9
Santa Cruz, CA 95060

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-02-061

Dear Ms Cook:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Since your request is general in nature and does not seek advice regarding a specific public official, we are treating your request as one for informal assistance.²

On August 17, 2001, John G. Barisone, Santa Cruz City Attorney, requested written advice from the Commission regarding the legality of proposed revisions to your city's campaign ordinance. (Barisone Advice Letter, No. I-01-201). Presently, you are requesting that the Commission render another advice letter on the same issue after we review additional revisions made to your city's ordinance.

We provide the following comments on the additional proposed revisions to the ordinance:

Section 2.10.030 provides certain benefits and incentives for accepting the contribution limit and voluntary expenditure option. New language was included in

¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
² Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Government Code section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section 18329(c)(3), copy enclosed.)

File No. 1-02-061
Page No. 2

subsections (6)(B)(3) and (4). This new language does not conflict with the provisions of
the Act.

Upon a second review of your ordinance, we affirm our advice provided to you in
our previous letter with the exception of asking you to replace all references to "the
Political Reform Act of 2001" with "the Political Reform Act of 1974, as amended in
2001." In an effort to keep your ordinance current with any future amendments to the
Political Reform Act, we suggest replacing all references to the Political Reform Act with
"the Political Reform Act, as amended" or more simply, "the Political Reform Act."

In addition, while not required due to conflicts with the Act, we offer the
following suggestions with regard to some provisions of your ordinance:

Section 2.10.030(a)(3) currently reads, in part, "…(without regard to whether or
not the candidate intends to make campaign expenditures in the minimum amount called
for by Political Reform Act of 1974, as amended in 2001, Chapter 4, Campaign
Disclosure);" We suggest replacement with "…(without regard to whether or not the
candidate intends to make campaign expenditures in the minimum amount called for by
the Political Reform Act of 1974, subdivision (a) of section 82013)." This section
specifies the minimum amount for qualification of a recipient committee.

Section 2.10.030(a)(6) might be better understood if written as follows: "on
behalf of the candidate or candidate's controlled committee, agrees to accept the
following conditions with respect to business and labor union contributions:"

Because the Commission is required to maintain current copies of all local
campaign ordinances, we would appreciate your forwarding a copy of your amended
ordinance once adopted. If you have any questions regarding this letter, please contact
me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

[signature]

By: Trish Mayer
Political Reform Consultant

sections.question_synthetic: Does proposed revisions to Santa Cruz's campaign ordinance comply with the Political Reform Act, and are there suggested improvements to the ordinance language?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The new language in Section 2.10.030 does not conflict with the Political Reform Act. The FPPC affirms its previous advice, recommends updating references to cite "the Political Reform Act, as amended," and offers non-binding suggestions for clarifying specific ordinance provisions.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION section headers but clearly presents a legal question and FPPC response. Identified as informal assistance (not formal opinion) per footnote 2. Previous related advice letter referenced (I-01-201).

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does proposed revisions to Santa Cruz's campaign ordinance comply with the Political Reform Act, and are there suggested improvements to the ordinance language?

CONCLUSION: The new language in Section 2.10.030 does not conflict with the Political Reform Act. The FPPC affirms its previous advice, recommends updating references to cite "the Political Reform Act, as amended," and offers non-binding suggestions for clarifying specific ordinance provisions.

embedding.summary: FPPC informal advice letter responding to Santa Cruz's request for review of additional proposed revisions to its campaign ordinance, affirming prior advice and providing technical suggestions for ordinance language improvements.
